outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump's 'Calm Before the Storm' posts signal looming Iran decision — experts alarmed
outlets.summary: CNN focuses on Trump's empty-handed return from Beijing and the imminent choice between renewed military strikes and continued stalled diplomacy. His 'to be continued!' Truth Social post is parsed as a deliberate signal that the military option remains live.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: As diplomacy collapses, US and Israel prepare for war's next chapter
outlets.summary: Iranian FM Araghchi declares US mistrust the main obstacle to any deal, warning of rising economic costs to Americans from the "war of choice" on Iran.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: IDF officer killed by unjammable Hezbollah drone, as Iran rebuilds missile defenses along Hormuz
outlets.summary: CNN reports on Capt. Recanati's death by fiber-optic FPV drone — immune to electronic jamming — alongside ISW's assessment that Iran has restored 30 of 33 Hormuz missile sites. Together the stories raise questions about whether the military situation is improving or worsening despite the ceasefire.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump posts 'calm before the storm' — Iran on notice as 24-hour decision clock ticks
outlets.summary: Fox News leads its Iran coverage with the NCRI rally near the US Capitol, framing thousands of Iranian diaspora demanding regime change as grassroots validation of the war's strategic goal. Coverage supports the view that Trump should intensify, not moderate, military pressure.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran announces Hormuz toll mechanism — will charge ships, ban US 'Project Freedom' vessels
outlets.summary: Fox covers the PGSA announcement as an Iranian provocation, framing it as Iran monetizing its control of the Strait and directly targeting Trump's Project Freedom escort mission.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: Shamkhani: Iran ready to sign nuclear deal but calls US control demands 'a fantasy'
outlets.summary: Khamenei adviser Shamkhani's comments are parsed by Daily Wire as confirmation that Iran will never fully dismantle its enrichment capability, validating continued pressure.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war live: Trump warns of 'very bad time'; Tehran eyes $2M Hormuz tolls in yuan
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with the PGSA toll mechanism announcement and Iran's mixed signal: Araghchi insists Tehran is ready for more talks even as Shamkhani calls US demands 'a fantasy' and the PGSA institutionalizes Hormuz control.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Iran's New Hormuz Authority Threatens to Cement Supply Disruption Beyond Any Ceasefire
outlets.summary: Bloomberg analysis warns that if the Persian Gulf Strait Authority becomes operational, it would structurally embed a Hormuz risk premium into global oil markets for years, regardless of when the conflict ends.

outlets.name: Euronews
outlets.headline: Iran threatens weapons-grade uranium enrichment as peace talks falter
outlets.summary: Euronews reports that Iranian MP Rezaei warned Iran could enrich uranium to 90% (weapons-grade) if the US resumes military strikes — a development that would trigger immediate Israeli/US military response.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Trump posts 'calm before the storm' as Israel braces for imminent decision on renewed Iran war
outlets.summary: The Times of Israel covers the PGSA announcement with analysis of what it means for Israeli-linked shipping, noting that vessels cooperating with Israel or the US would be excluded from the fee-based transit system.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF confirms killing Hamas military chief Haddad; Netanyahu: "last architect of Oct. 7" eliminated
outlets.summary: The Times of Israel leads with the confirmed killing of Izz al-Din al-Haddad, describing the strike as using 13 munitions on the Gaza City building and noting that Netanyahu personally ordered it.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: U.S. and Israel preparing for possible renewal of strikes on Iran as early as next week
outlets.summary: Haaretz broke the story on US-Israel 'intense preparations' for possible renewed strikes on Iran as early as next week, citing options including Kharg Island seizure and nuclear material extraction commando missions. The report signals diplomacy is effectively over unless Iran moves sharply.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi: All countries acknowledge Iran was the victor — we prevented enemies from achieving their goals
outlets.summary: PressTV frames the PGSA as Iran legitimately exercising sovereign rights over the strait, presenting the fee mechanism as a rational and proportionate response to the US blockade.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi: Americans are told they must absorb rocketing costs of war of choice on Iran
outlets.summary: PressTV amplifies Araghchi's BRICS speech warning US citizens about the economic toll of the Iran war, framing gas prices, mortgage rates and stock volatility as consequences of US aggression.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Shamkhani: Iran ready to sign nuclear deal — if US removes all sanctions immediately
outlets.summary: Iran International (opposition channel) covers Shamkhani's conditional offer skeptically, noting the 'quick removal of all sanctions' condition is one Washington has repeatedly rejected as a starting point.
